title: Particle Transformer for Jet Tagging
abstract: Jet tagging is a critical task in high-energy physics. We propose Particle Transformer (ParT), a model based on the transformer architecture using particle interactions as additional inputs. ParT achieves new state-of-the-art performance on two widely-used jet tagging benchmarks: top quark tagging and quark-gluon discrimination.

title: ParticleNet: Jet Tagging via Particle Clouds
abstract: We propose ParticleNet, a customized neural network architecture using the concept of particle cloud—an unordered set of particles—for jet tagging. ParticleNet achieves state-of-the-art performance on top quark and quark-gluon tagging tasks, significantly outperforming previous methods.

title: Energy Flow Networks: Deep Sets for Particle Jets
abstract: We introduce Energy Flow Networks (EFNs) and Particle Flow Networks (PFNs), two infrastructures for building jet classifiers based on the energy flow of the event. EFNs and PFNs are both infrared and collinear safe by construction. We demonstrate their performance on quark-gluon discrimination and top quark tagging.

title: PELICAN: Permutation Equivariant and Lorentz Invariant or Covariant Aggregator Network for Jet Physics
abstract: We introduce PELICAN, a neural network architecture that is equivariant under permutations of the input particles and equivariant (or invariant) under Lorentz symmetry. PELICAN achieves state-of-the-art performance on top quark tagging with fewer parameters than competing architectures.

title: LorentzNet: Lorentz Group Equivariant Graph Neural Networks for Jet Tagging
abstract: We present LorentzNet, a graph neural network architecture that is equivariant under the Lorentz group. By exploiting Lorentz symmetry, LorentzNet achieves state-of-the-art performance on top quark tagging with significantly fewer parameters than transformer-based models.

title: Fourier Neural Operator for Parametric Partial Differential Equations
abstract: We propose the Fourier neural operator (FNO) to learn mappings between function spaces. We formulate the neural operator directly in Fourier space, enabling an expressive and efficient architecture. We evaluate on Navier-Stokes turbulence and demonstrate significant improvements over existing neural operators.

title: Pangu-Weather: A 3D High-Resolution Model for Fast and Accurate Global Weather Forecast
abstract: We present Pangu-Weather, a deep learning based method for fast and accurate global weather forecast. Pangu-Weather employs a 3D Earth transformer architecture and hierarchical temporal aggregation. Pangu-Weather achieves more accurate deterministic forecasts than the operational IFS model across all tested variables.

title: GraphCast: Learning skillful medium-range global weather forecasting
abstract: We introduce GraphCast, a machine learning weather prediction system based on graph neural networks. GraphCast is trained on historical weather data to make predictions for many weather variables over the next 10 days at 0.25° resolution. GraphCast significantly outperforms the most accurate operational deterministic forecast systems.

title: Learning Local Equivariant Representations for Large-Scale Atomistic Dynamics
abstract: We present MACE, a higher-order message passing neural network for fast and accurate force fields. MACE achieves state-of-the-art accuracy on the MD17 benchmark and shows unprecedented performance on the 3BPA benchmark, demonstrating exceptional data efficiency and transferability.

title: E(3)-equivariant graph neural networks for data-efficient and accurate interatomic potentials
abstract: We introduce NequIP, an E(3)-equivariant neural network approach for learning interatomic potentials from quantum-mechanical calculations. NequIP achieves state-of-the-art accuracy on the MD17 benchmark while requiring significantly less training data than competing methods.
